interfaces.name: lima.LimaVM._generate_lima_config — opencode mounts
interfaces.behavior_change: When 'opencode' in self.config.frameworks, append two host mounts: ~/.config/opencode -> /home/<user>.linux/.config/opencode (writable) and ~/.local/share/opencode -> /home/<user>.linux/.local/share/opencode (writable). Use mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True) on the host paths first (matches the claude_dir.mkdir(exist_ok=True) pattern). When opencode is not in frameworks, no mounts are added.

interfaces.name: config.Config.opencode_version (new field)
interfaces.behavior_change: New dataclass field parallel to claude_code_version and codex_version. Config.load reads versions.opencode via _validate_version_pin('opencode', raw_versions.get('opencode')) and stores on the instance. Config.save emits versions['opencode'] = self.opencode_version when pinned.

interfaces.name: cli._get_latest_opencode_version (new helper)
interfaces.behavior_change: Host curl against https://api.github.com/repos/anomalyco/opencode/releases/latest; parse tag_name (e.g. 'v1.14.33'); strip the leading 'v'; return the bare version. Returns None on any failure (timeout, non-200, JSON parse error, missing tag_name) — matches the existing _get_npm_latest_version skip-on-failure pattern.

interfaces.name: cli._update_opencode (new helper)
interfaces.behavior_change: Run the opencode install script inside the VM via limactl shell, with OPENCODE_INSTALL_DIR=$HOME/.local/bin and OPENCODE_VERSION=<version_str>; --no-modify-path. Returns True iff returncode == 0.

interfaces.name: cli._resolve_framework_versions — opencode branch
interfaces.behavior_change: When 'opencode' in config.frameworks: if config.opencode_version, use it; else call _get_latest_opencode_version() (host curl, NOT npm-in-VM). Store under resolved['opencode'].

interfaces.name: cli._check_library_updates — opencode comparison
interfaces.behavior_change: When 'opencode' in desired and desired['opencode']: get installed via _get_vm_tool_version(vm, 'opencode --version'); compare; if different, append to changes list. On user confirmation, dispatch via kind == 'opencode' to _update_opencode.

interfaces.name: provisioner._generate_playbook — opencode_version Ansible var
interfaces.behavior_change: Add 'opencode_version': self.config.opencode_version or 'latest' to the vars dict, parallel to codex_version. Story 01's role already accepts the literal 'latest' sentinel; this story wires the Config pin through.

data_flow: User pins versions.opencode in .clauded.yaml -> Config.load validates and stores Config.opencode_version -> Provisioner._generate_playbook passes opencode_version Ansible var to the role -> role uses pin or falls back to GitHub API resolution. Independently: cli main flow runs _check_library_updates -> calls _resolve_framework_versions which adds an 'opencode' entry (pin or _get_latest_opencode_version()) -> compares to _get_vm_tool_version(vm, 'opencode --version') -> if different, prompts user -> dispatch to _update_opencode which runs the install script in the VM. lima.py mount block: when 'opencode' in config.frameworks, two host mounts ensure auth tokens, MCP OAuth, sessions, and TUI prefs persist across VM destroy/recreate cycles.
files_to_modify: src/clauded/lima.py — add opencode mount block in _generate_lima_config (conditional on 'opencode' in frameworks)
src/clauded/config.py — add Config.opencode_version field, parse versions.opencode in load, emit versions.opencode in save
src/clauded/cli.py — new _get_latest_opencode_version (host curl), new _update_opencode (in-VM install script), extend _resolve_framework_versions, extend _check_library_updates
src/clauded/provisioner.py — add 'opencode_version' Ansible var line in _generate_playbook (deferred from Story 01)
tests/test_lima.py — paired include/exclude for opencode mounts (mkdir called on both host paths when opencode in frameworks; not called when absent)
tests/test_config.py — round-trip for opencode_version pin; reject bogus pins via _validate_version_pin

tests/test_version_check.py — opencode update detected (mock GitHub API + _get_vm_tool_version); pin overrides latest; _get_latest_opencode_version parses tag_name; _get_latest_opencode_version returns None on failure
CHANGELOG.md — [Unreleased] Added entry for opencode mount points, opencode update check, Config.opencode_version pin
implementation_strategy:
TDD per-component:

1. RED: lima.py mount tests (paired include/exclude for opencode mounts; mkdir assertions).
2. GREEN: lima.py — append two opencode mount entries when 'opencode' in self.config.frameworks; mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True) on both host paths.
3. RED: config.py opencode_version round-trip + validation tests.
4. GREEN: config.py — add field, load (raw_versions.get('opencode')), save (versions['opencode'] when pinned).
5. RED: cli.py update-check tests (4 cases: detected, pin overrides latest, GitHub parse, GitHub failure).
6. GREEN: cli.py — _get_latest_opencode_version, _update_opencode, _resolve_framework_versions extension, _check_library_updates extension.
7. provisioner.py — add the 'opencode_version' Ansible var line; reuse Story 01's role default.
8. Add CHANGELOG entry.
9. make check; coverage >= 80%; all tests green.

Mock strategy:
- function-boundary-patch on _get_latest_opencode_version and _get_vm_tool_version for update-check tests.
- subprocess.run mock for _get_latest_opencode_version (GitHub API curl call) — return a fixture JSON body with tag_name = 'v1.14.33'.
- For mkdir assertions in lima tests: patch Path.mkdir at module boundary.
- isolated-filesystem-yaml for round-trip tests.
